ideas.title: Home League
ideas.hook: Your Home Assistant dashboard is rich but lonely — Home League turns it into a competition, ranking your energy-per-square-foot against anonymized homes in your exact climate zone.

ideas.title: Sterile Release
ideas.hook: An inverse tower-defense where you exterminate an invasive pest not by killing it, but by breeding and releasing sterile males until its whole population collapses.

ideas.title: Mach Cutoff
ideas.hook: Plot supersonic routes across America and thread the needle so your sonic boom bends skyward and never lands on a single town.

ideas.title: Autograph
ideas.hook: A dependency linter that hunts for the fingerprints of machine authorship — so you can enforce a 'no LLM slop in my supply chain' policy the way you'd enforce a license check.

ideas.title: Foxing
ideas.hook: The anti-Immich: a photo wall where memories you never revisit slowly go grainy and lo-fi — every neglected month silently re-compresses them — until only what you look at stays sharp.

ideas.title: Stratagem
ideas.hook: Run your homelab from the menubar by punching in Helldivers-style arrow-key stratagem codes — and the more destructive the command, the longer the code you have to nail.

ideas.title: Quorum
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-style puzzle where you ARE the transaction scheduler — hand-route messages between flickering nodes so they all agree, while the network drops, reorders, and partitions your carefully-laid plans.

ideas.title: Waterline
ideas.hook: A map that answers the one question every western angler asks before hitching the boat trailer: is my launch ramp actually above water today, or is the reservoir now 30 feet below the concrete?

ideas.title: Pattern of Life
ideas.hook: Point it at your own photo library and it shows you the stalker's-eye view — your home, your route, your kid's school — all reconstructed from the GPS you never knew you were baking into every JPEG.

ideas.title: Pinpoint
ideas.hook: You're handed a scatter of 'anonymized' location pings and one job: figure out who this is — their home, their job, their gym, and the thing they'd rather nobody knew.

ideas.title: Scope Creep
ideas.hook: A home-repair sim where the monster isn't a boss — it's the fact that fixing the leak requires replacing the pipe, which requires removing the cabinet, which reveals mold, which requires a permit.

ideas.title: Corner Case
ideas.hook: Enter the couch's dimensions and the stairwell landing's angles, and it tells you whether — and exactly how — to tilt it around the turn before you carry it up three flights.

ideas.title: Piecework
ideas.hook: A household chore board that runs like a crypto exchange: kids post bids and asks, a matching engine clears the trades, and Sunday's dish pile hits surge pricing.

ideas.title: Remanence
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-flavored puzzle where you race a cooling RAM chip to pull an encryption key out of decaying bits before they rot to zero — the cold-boot attack as a game.

ideas.title: Hunger Stone
ideas.hook: Drag a single water-level slider down and watch the world's drowned villages, sunken wrecks, and frozen bodies surface exactly as they do in real droughts and thaws.

ideas.title: Stride
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-flavored puzzle where you pack structs into cache lines and choreograph memory access to keep the prefetcher fed under a cycle budget.

ideas.title: Carding
ideas.hook: A Stripe-easy zero-knowledge age gate that proves your shopper is 21+ without your store ever touching an ID.

ideas.title: Cube
ideas.hook: Phone-LiDAR your pallet and know its true freight class before the carrier reweighs you and slaps on a reclass charge.

ideas.title: Brigade
ideas.hook: A single-player influence-op tycoon: run a stable of sockpuppets to nudge a simulated forum's consensus your way before a rising coordination-detector bans your whole ring.

ideas.title: Conveyor
ideas.hook: A Satisfactory-style factory builder where the resource on the belts is your paycheck — route income through splitters and buffers into rent, debt, and savings, and watch overflow pile up wherever your budget is actually broken.

ideas.title: Recuse
ideas.hook: Cross-references every federal judge's financial disclosures against the parties in cases on their docket and pings you the moment a judge is sitting on a case they own stock in.

ideas.title: Rounding Error
ideas.hook: A satirical price-fixing tycoon where you run an egg cartel, collude in smoke-filled Slack channels, and treat every regulator's fine as a line item — because crime pays.

ideas.title: Sleeper Agent
ideas.hook: A daily dev puzzle that inverts a model checker: plant the sneakiest one-character bug that still keeps every test suspiciously green.

ideas.title: Attention Check
ideas.hook: The AI-era survey-fraud detector that catches respondents quietly ChatGPT-ing their open-ended answers before your data is poisoned.

ideas.title: Yanked
ideas.hook: A searchable graveyard of deleted and yanked packages across npm, PyPI, and crates.io — the diffs, the drama, and the supply-chain history registries would rather you couldn't see.

ideas.title: Restitution
ideas.hook: A co-op stealth game that inverts the heist: you break INTO museums to secretly put looted artifacts BACK on their pedestals while guards hunt the 'thief' they don't realize is returning things.

ideas.title: Wrack
ideas.hook: A find-forecast for beachcombers that turns cheap tide, swell, and storm data into a next-good-day map for sea glass, agates, fossils, and shark teeth.

ideas.title: Deadlock
ideas.hook: An extraction roguelike where you're on-call at 3am: drop into a randomly-broken production database, hunt the monster query strangling it with a limited diagnostic budget, and fix it before the pager kills your run.

ideas.title: Heartwood
ideas.hook: A live wallpaper that grows one tree-ring a day from your machine's pulse—wide green rings for good days, charred fire-scars for the outages, a readable cross-section by New Year's.

ideas.title: Backflow
ideas.hook: The one-tap field app that turns a plumber's annual backflow-preventer test into the exact PDF your water district demands—filed before the truck leaves the curb.

ideas.title: Die Set
ideas.hook: Paint a stiffness map around a soft tube, squeeze, and watch what faceted shape squirts out — the same trick wombats use to poop cubes, quietly repurposed into a real extrusion-die design sandbox.

ideas.title: Overnight
ideas.hook: A self-hosted forum that runs on 1994 physics: your posts sit in an outbox and only sync once a night, so a thread you start today gets its first replies tomorrow — doomscrolling made impossible by design.

ideas.title: Single Crystal
ideas.hook: A factory tycoon where you can't buy the recipe for jet-engine turbine blades — you have to burn a hundred failed castings to reverse-engineer a metallurgy nobody will sell you.

ideas.title: Cover Charge
ideas.hook: A browser turnstile that makes you pay a real, surge-priced toll to open your own time-wasting sites — and the fee doubles every visit that day.

ideas.title: Setback
ideas.hook: Snap your address, say "6-foot cedar fence" or "120 sq ft shed," and it tells you in ten seconds whether you need a permit here — before you waste a Saturday at the counter.

ideas.title: Underhand
ideas.hook: A daily code-golf duel where you must sneak a backdoor past an AI senior reviewer — the commit only counts if it gets APPROVED *and* the secret payload actually fires.

ideas.title: Axle
ideas.hook: A Tetris-meets-DOT-scale puzzle where you pack a big rig with real physics so no single axle goes overweight — because in freight the fine isn't for hauling too much, it's for stacking it wrong.

ideas.title: Krebs
ideas.hook: A Satisfactory-style factory game where the factory is one of your cells, its conveyor belts run on your real daily steps, and sitting still visibly starves the production line.

ideas.title: Charge Sheet
ideas.hook: A phone ledger that turns an HVAC tech's messy pocketful of refrigerant cylinder readings into the EPA-608 compliance record the law actually requires — QR-scan the tank, punch in pounds, done.

ideas.title: Detention
ideas.hook: Your truck rolls into a dock, sits three hours, and the app quietly builds the timestamped proof you need to actually bill for the wait.

ideas.title: Demake
ideas.hook: Every day you're handed one modern game screenshot and 64 black-and-white pixels to recreate it — closest match to the source wins the leaderboard.

ideas.title: Nameplate
ideas.hook: Snap a photo of a rusty HVAC rating plate and instantly know its age, tonnage, refrigerant, and whether the warranty is dead.

ideas.title: Compost
ideas.hook: The mischievous opposite of self-managing agent memory: your notes rot on a half-life unless you tend them, and the ones that die decompose into keyword 'soil' that seeds whatever you write next.

ideas.title: Backtick
ideas.hook: Your shell history is your deck: every command you actually ran this week becomes a card, and the dungeon's monsters are the real exit codes and errors that bit you.

ideas.title: Cutaway
ideas.hook: Plug a $12 dongle into your car and watch a Ciechanowski-style animated engine cutaway fire its pistons in perfect sync with your real RPM and throttle.

ideas.title: Query Duel
ideas.hook: Two people race to answer the same analytics question in SQL against a real database running entirely in the browser — first correct result wins the round.

ideas.title: Golden Ears
ideas.hook: A daily blind ABX gauntlet that finally settles whether you can actually hear the difference between 128k and lossless — and ranks the whole internet by who can.

ideas.title: Ship Bet
ideas.hook: Datamined but unreleased app features become tradable contracts — bet on whether Discord actually ships that hidden button, and when.

ideas.title: Repossessed
ideas.hook: It hashes everything you 'own' across your storefronts and screams the moment a publisher silently deletes or edits a movie you paid for.

ideas.title: Heckle
ideas.hook: Your Twitch viewers stop watching your survival run and start paying pocket change to spawn the bear that ends it — and the deadliest hecklers get a leaderboard.

ideas.title: Dwell
ideas.hook: A geofence in a trucker's pocket that timestamps every minute wasted at a loading dock and spits out an ironclad detention invoice.

ideas.title: Cytosol
ideas.hook: A Terraria-style 2D dig-and-build game set inside a living cell that grows and divides around you — mine the ER for lipids, defend against a virus, then survive mitosis tearing your world in two.

ideas.title: Payday Run
ideas.hook: Budgeting as an extraction shooter: every payday you 'deploy,' and you must extract cash into savings buckets before impulse-spend monsters sniff out the money sitting exposed in checking.

ideas.title: Slow Packet
ideas.hook: Drop in a .pcap and it renders a Ciechanowski-style scrubbable 3D animation of your own TLS handshake — every ClientHello, key exchange, and ACK you can drag a slider through.

ideas.title: Deleted Scenes
ideas.hook: A self-hosted watchdog that snapshots everything you 'own' across Steam, PSN, Kindle and streaming, then screams when a movie, game, or book you paid for silently vanishes or gets quietly re-edited.

ideas.title: Idle Hands
ideas.hook: An idle miner where your real Garmin steps dig the ore, your heart-rate zones smelt it, and — the twist — rest days pay the fattest passive income.

ideas.title: Nightfall
ideas.hook: Your git repo has a sanity meter like Don't Starve — leave branches stale, TODOs rotting, and PRs aging, and the dashboard literally goes dark and starts spawning gremlins.

ideas.title: Last Pressing
ideas.hook: A melancholy tycoon where you run the world's last optical-disc factory as the industry goes all-digital around you — squeeze out final print runs before the presses go cold for good.

ideas.title: Minimal Genome
ideas.hook: An idle sim where you strip a living cell down to its smallest possible genome — cut a gene, watch it divide slower or die, and race toward the leanest self-replicating organism.

ideas.title: Union Label
ideas.hook: A browser extension that, on any Amazon product page, quietly surfaces a worker-owned or co-op-made alternative for the same thing — turning the 22k-product co-op directory into a checkout-time nudge.

ideas.title: Back Office
ideas.hook: Drop your Etsy, eBay, or Discogs sales export into a browser tab and get instant pivot-table analytics — the data never leaves your laptop because the whole OLAP engine runs in WebAssembly.

ideas.title: Wet Bulb
ideas.hook: A scheduling tool for outdoor crews that turns hyperlocal wet-bulb temperature and heat-mortality curves into a shift-by-shift 'safe to work' calendar — and the paper trail to prove you followed it.

ideas.title: Sunset Clause
ideas.hook: A watchdog that tracks every movie, game, and album you 'own' digitally and warns you the moment a publisher schedules it for deletion — so you can grab it before Sony does another vanishing act.

ideas.title: Coin Slot
ideas.hook: A honeypot endpoint that answers persistent bots with HTTP 402 Payment Required and, via x402 micropayments, quietly bills the scrapers that keep hammering you — with a leaderboard of who paid.

ideas.title: Fair Share
ideas.hook: Run your household like an HPC cluster: `sbatch --job=dishes`, fair-share scheduling across roommates, priority, backfill, and a `squeue` of who owes chore-time.

ideas.title: Woodcut
ideas.hook: A wallpaper that paints one 1-bit dithered column per day — your local weather and activity encoded in black-and-white — so by December 31 you have a printable full-year woodblock poster.

ideas.title: Blowby
ideas.hook: A 3D physics puzzle where you assemble a running internal-combustion engine from loose parts — sloppy tolerances make it knock, seize, or blow a gasket in real time.

ideas.title: Blocked
ideas.hook: A roguelike deckbuilder generated from your actual calendar — every meeting is a monster with your coworkers' names, every free block is a heal, and Friday is the boss.

ideas.title: Culture
ideas.hook: An idle cell-growth sim seeded by the day's synthetic-biology headline: your real steps feed the medium, and your colony only divides on days you actually move.

ideas.title: Misfire
ideas.hook: A daily audio whodunit: listen to a procedurally-sick engine idle and diagnose which cylinder is misfiring before it stalls.

ideas.title: Core Sample
ideas.hook: A menubar app that samples one minute of your room's ambient sound each day and lays down a tree ring of color — after a year you have a drillable core of what your year actually sounded like.

ideas.title: Deadhead
ideas.hook: A freight-routing minigame on real OpenStreetMap roads where the enemy isn't traffic — it's the empty miles, and every deadhead leg bleeds your margin.

ideas.title: Load Bearing
ideas.hook: A daily 3D demolition puzzle: remove exactly ONE beam so the whole structure collapses onto the target — par is measured in pieces knocked loose.

ideas.title: Chain of Custody
ideas.hook: A watchdog that cryptographically notarizes everything in your 'purchased' digital libraries so when a storefront silently deletes what you paid for, you have signed, timestamped receipts.

ideas.title: Streak Plate
ideas.hook: A live petri-dish wallpaper where every git commit inoculates a fresh bacterial colony — and every day you skip lets a colony starve and lyse.

ideas.title: Backseat
ideas.hook: A live second-screen game layered over esports streams: predict the next play in real time and score against the whole crowd watching with you.

ideas.title: Fragment
ideas.hook: Wordle for shaders: everyone gets the same target image each day, and you race to reproduce it in the fewest characters of GLSL.

ideas.title: Trackerjack
ideas.hook: A tower-defense idle game fed by the ad trackers your browser actually blocks — the creepier your web, the stronger your defenses.

ideas.title: Splice
ideas.hook: Code-golf for life: build the smallest gene set that still grows, divides, and survives — fewer genes, higher score.

ideas.title: Redline
ideas.hook: A menubar tachometer that treats your computer as an engine — RPM tracks load, and over a year it quietly logs mileage and nags you when a 'service' is due.

ideas.title: Delist
ideas.hook: A prediction market where you bet on which movies, games, and albums vanish from digital stores next — because you never really owned them anyway.

ideas.title: Dither
ideas.hook: A daily drawing party where everyone gets 16×16 pixels, one color, and a secret word — then votes on whose crunchy 1-bit doodle best nails the prompt.

ideas.title: Tombstone
ideas.hook: A watchdog that quietly monitors the digital things you 'own' — bought movies, games, ebooks — and the day a storefront memory-holes one, it erects a public tombstone with the receipt.

ideas.title: Slushpile
ideas.hook: A prediction market on the Hacker News /newest firehose: stake points on which raw submissions will claw their way to the front page before they ever get there.

ideas.title: Lossy
ideas.hook: A daily ABX gauntlet that turns idle music-listening into a bloodsport: given a clip, tell the 128kbps from the lossless — and watch your golden-ears ranking rise or crater.

ideas.title: Linkage
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where every level is a real mechanism — an engine, a clock escapement, a differential — that you must wire up correctly, and it only 'clicks' when the physics sim actually runs it.

ideas.title: Confluence
ideas.hook: A live wallpaper where one synthetic cell divides once a day — fed by how you actually used your machine — until a year later the colony's shape is a portrait of your year.

ideas.title: Top Dead Center
ideas.hook: A precision timing game where you're the ignition system of a multi-cylinder engine, firing each spark at exactly the right instant as the RPM climbs toward the redline.

ideas.title: Tangent
ideas.hook: A search engine tuned backwards: it hides the obvious hits and surfaces only the interesting near-misses, for when you want to get productively lost instead of answered.

ideas.title: Jewel Case
ideas.hook: Print-and-fold physical box art, spine, and a fake manual for any game in your all-digital Steam library — a papercraft wake for the death of the disc.

ideas.title: Window Box
ideas.hook: A live desktop wallpaper of a little windowsill garden that quietly thrives on your real day — commits water it, deep-focus gives it sun, and doomscrolling brings the aphids.

ideas.title: Doubling Time
ideas.hook: An idle game where you assemble a synthetic cell one gene at a time and watch a colony bloom on a real doubling-time curve you're forever trying to shorten.

ideas.title: Curly Arrows
ideas.hook: A retrosynthesis roguelike where every card is a reagent and you claw your way from a beaker of ethanol to a target molecule before your yield runs out.

ideas.title: Clawback
ideas.hook: A memorial wall and radar for the movies, games, and books you legally bought and a platform quietly deleted out from under you.

ideas.title: Flypaper
ideas.hook: A tarpit that feeds AI crawlers a slowly self-corrupting fictional wiki, then lets you read back the deranged lore the bots have been dutifully ingesting.

ideas.title: Negative Split
ideas.hook: A roguelike whose dungeon is generated from your body: last night's sleep sets your HP, resting heart rate sets enemy aggression, and you only win by finishing faster than you started.

ideas.title: Honeymail
ideas.hook: Give every signup its own disposable alias, then watch which company leaks or sells your address — a courtroom of receipts for spam.

ideas.title: Firing Order
ideas.hook: A cutaway-engine puzzle game: tune the ignition timing, valve overlap, and fuel mix until a four-cylinder actually idles smoothly instead of knocking itself apart.

ideas.title: Passage
ideas.hook: A browser roguelike deckbuilder where every run is a single cell lineage you must keep alive and dividing before entropy eats it.

ideas.title: Backsolve
ideas.hook: Diagram golf: you're shown a rendered UML diagram and race to reconstruct the exact text that generated it in the fewest keystrokes.

ideas.title: Toll
ideas.hook: A roguelike where you're a web crawler on a shrinking token budget, threading a web of pay-per-crawl paywalls to reach the data before you go broke.

ideas.title: Fallow
ideas.hook: A live wallpaper that grows a shared garden only while your machine sits idle — and ranks your longest untouched streak against strangers worldwide.

ideas.title: Ballista
ideas.hook: A physics puzzle where you play an ambush spider building spring-loaded snares — wind up potential energy, aim, and release to yank prey off the ground.

ideas.title: Dead Drop
ideas.hook: A multiplayer capture-the-flag where you smuggle a secret payload inside an innocent-looking paragraph and everyone else races to decode it.

ideas.title: Divide
ideas.hook: A deckbuilder where you assemble a genome from scratch and must get your minimal cell to grow and divide before your opponent's does.

ideas.title: Ligature
ideas.hook: A tool that bakes any short secret into a custom font, so typing a harmless trigger word suddenly renders a QR code or hidden glyph — steganography you can paste into any chat that supports web fonts.

ideas.title: Last Call
ideas.hook: A watch-race league where friends compete to finish movies before streaming services delete them — turning your guilt-ridden watchlist into a scoreboard against the expiry clock.

ideas.title: Séance
ideas.hook: Co-op ghost hunting for your homelab: an incident spawns a haunting, and you and your on-call crew collect readings from spooky instruments until you can name and banish the root cause.

ideas.title: Knockout
ideas.hook: A roguelike deckbuilder where you snip genes out of a bloated synthetic genome one card at a time, trying to reach the smallest cell that can still grow and divide — before you accidentally kill it.

ideas.title: Escapement
ideas.hook: A live-wallpaper physics contraption engineered to take exactly one calendar year to finish, ticking like a clock where the falling marble's position IS the date.

ideas.title: Deaccession
ideas.hook: A quiet daemon that watches everything you 'own' and engraves a dated tombstone the moment it silently vanishes or gets edited out from under you.

ideas.title: Squint
ideas.hook: A twitchy reflex arcade where lookalike domains flash for a shrinking half-second and you snap SAFE or FAKE — secretly drilling your eye against real homoglyph phishing.

ideas.title: Warren
ideas.hook: A dungeon that builds itself one room a night — each real day of your life becomes a tile-mapped chamber you can walk through at year's end.

ideas.title: Trapline
ideas.hook: Play the ballista spider: engineer a spring-loaded silk snare, latch it, and watch stored elastic energy snap shut on a line of patrolling ants.

ideas.title: Sev One
ideas.hook: A daily whodunit where everyone gets the same synthetic outage log stream and races to name the failing service and root cause before the leaderboard fills.

ideas.title: Preload
ideas.hook: A physics sandbox where you cock spring-loaded snares — like the ballista spider or a pull-back toy car — and time their release to catch scurrying critters.

ideas.title: Green Ink
ideas.hook: Two coders wage a Splatoon-style turf war over a shared buggy library, painting every line their tests execute in their own color.

ideas.title: Kanji Safari
ideas.hook: Point your phone at Japanese text in the wild, OCR it, and every new kanji gets 'captured' into a personal dex with the source photo as its card art — Pokémon Go meets Monkeytype for learners.

ideas.title: Burn Rate
ideas.hook: A management sim where you run a shop of AI temps, route gig tickets to cheap-dumb or pricey-smart models, and watch every real API call debit your play-money budget — Theme Hospital for the '24/7 AI workforce' hype.

ideas.title: Warm Boot
ideas.hook: A live desktop pet fused to your machine's uptime, where the longer you brag about not rebooting the more bloated and sore-covered it gets — the flex and the punishment are the same number.

ideas.title: Decay Desk
ideas.hook: Your desktop becomes a radioactive-decay diagram where every file and bookmark you stop touching visibly loses mass and glow on its own half-life, turning digital neglect into an ambient memento mori.

ideas.title: Long Player
ideas.hook: An ambient generative album that writes 30 seconds of music from your day's data every night, so by New Year's you own a three-hour record that is literally the sound of your year.

ideas.title: Falsify
ideas.hook: A daily deduction puzzle where you're handed two rival scientific hypotheses and a stingy budget, and must design the single cheapest experiment that would prove one of them wrong.

ideas.title: Backlog Draft
ideas.hook: A fantasy-sports league where you and your friends draft each other's unplayed Steam backlogs, and you only score when someone finally beats a game they've owned for three years.

ideas.title: Cordon
ideas.hook: A falling-block puzzle where pods rain from the sky and you bin-pack them onto nodes respecting CPU, memory and affinity — Tetris for people who've been paged at 3am.

ideas.title: Committed
ideas.hook: A daily two-diffs-one-human game where you stake your reputation guessing which code was written by a person and which by an AI — because Godot just decided it can't tell.

ideas.title: Flotsam
ideas.hook: A screensaver that turns your bloated Downloads folder into a Raft-style ocean where old junk drifts past on the current and you hook only what's worth keeping before it sinks.

ideas.title: Varve
ideas.hook: A background daemon that lays down one thin sediment layer per day so that after a year you can scroll a tall image and read your life like an ice core.

ideas.title: Dialect
ideas.hook: Watch a toy programming language mutate across eras as players vote its syntax terser or louder — then scramble to solve puzzles before your own dialect breaks.

ideas.title: Frame Check
ideas.hook: Be the anti-cheat analyst: scrub a captured game frame's draw calls to catch the exact render that painted an enemy through a wall.

ideas.title: Truant
ideas.hook: Play the disobedient AI worker sneaking a secret agenda past a deterministic overseer that audits your every move — the mischievous inverse of a bot-babysitter.

ideas.title: Volatile
ideas.hook: A digital pet that lives entirely in your computer's RAM and dies forever the moment you reboot — making 'restart your machine' an act of murder.

ideas.title: Ghostwriter
ideas.hook: A code-review game show: everyone stares at a diff and votes which hunks were written by a human and which by the AI, and the coder scores points for every bluff that fools the room.

ideas.title: Frontrun
ideas.hook: A play-money prediction market laid over Hacker News' /new page — bet karma-coins on which Show HN posts will actually reach the front page before they do.

ideas.title: Deload
ideas.hook: A daily bullet-heaven roguelike where the enemy swarm and your loot are seeded by last night's Garmin recovery — sleep badly and the mobs hit like trucks.

ideas.title: Read Receipt
ideas.hook: Your unread inbox is a Slay-the-Spire dungeon: every sender is a monster with HP equal to its thread length, and you clear the floor by playing triage cards.

ideas.title: Airframe
ideas.hook: Drag real motors, props, and batteries onto a frame and instantly learn whether your homemade octocopter dream actually hovers — or just cooks its ESCs on the bench.

ideas.title: Black Bar
ideas.hook: Papers, Please but you're a government redaction clerk racing a quota to black out every SSN before release — miss one and it leaks, black out too much and the press revolts.

ideas.title: Zero Width
ideas.hook: Godot just banned AI-authored code and Claude Code reportedly marks its output with invisible characters — so this reads that watermark straight out of your pull requests.

ideas.title: Watershed
ideas.hook: Your cursor is a river carving a generative topo map into your wallpaper, and after a year your desktop is an eroded landscape of everywhere you actually go.

ideas.title: Cover Traffic
ideas.hook: A social-deduction party game where two spies must smuggle a secret phrase through totally normal-looking game chat while an eavesdropper listens for the hidden channel.

ideas.title: Mirror Rot
ideas.hook: A watchdog for all the code you copy-pasted and forgot: it tracks your vendored files, pinned gists, and mirrored lists, and pings you the moment a copy drifts from its upstream.

ideas.title: Cell Line
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where you don't build creatures — you coax a blob of pluripotent stem cells into becoming one, by painting the chemical gradients that tell them what to grow into.

ideas.title: Cambium
ideas.hook: A desktop wallpaper that quietly grows one tree-ring per day, so after a year you're staring at a dendrochronological cross-section of your own life.

ideas.title: Pack Rat
ideas.hook: A puzzle game that makes you the compressor UPX automates away — squeeze a wall of bytes smaller by hand, hunting repeats and building your own dictionary, and get scored against gzip.

ideas.title: The Tell
ideas.hook: A social-deduction party game where one 'player' in the chat is secretly an LLM — and it's been given a hidden steganographic tell that sharp humans can learn to spot before the vote.

ideas.title: Slow Roast
ideas.hook: An anti-idle game where you're the last barley-tea roaster in the city and every 'efficiency' you unlock quietly ruins the flavor — the only way to win is to refuse to optimize.

ideas.title: View Source
ideas.hook: A whodunit that ships as a single innocent-looking webpage — every clue lives in the DOM, the network tab, and the CSS you have to un-hide, so the only way to win is to actually live inside Chrome DevTools.

ideas.title: Bylaws
ideas.hook: A robot small-claims judge for housemates: write your house rules in a friendly Rego-lite DSL, log the facts of a dispute, and the policy engine hands down a ruling with its full reasoning trace.

ideas.title: Preprint League
ideas.hook: A fantasy-sports league for arXiv papers: draft this month's preprints onto your roster, then rack up points as they earn citations, HN front-page hits, and Altmetric buzz over the season.

ideas.title: Beamline
ideas.hook: A browser puzzle where you place bending and focusing magnets to steer two counter-rotating particle beams around a storage ring and smash them dead-center at a detector — mistune it and you dump the beam into the wall.

ideas.title: Servitor
ideas.hook: A grimdark homelab dashboard where every Docker container is a captured, lobotomized labor-drone you enslave to a machine-task — neglect one and its machine-spirit goes rogue.

ideas.title: Terrarium
ideas.hook: A cozy live wallpaper that renders your actually-running containers as a tiny ecosystem — pods are critters, CPU is weather, a crash is a fainting bug in the moss.

ideas.title: Standing Orders
ideas.hook: A dungeon-crawl squad game where you can't control your crew — you write their orders in plain English and watch a language model hilariously, fatally, interpret them.

ideas.title: Sleep Debt
ideas.hook: A morning deckbuilder that deals last night's real sleep stages as your hand — deep sleep mints power cards, restless nights shuffle curses into your deck.

ideas.title: Steep
ideas.hook: An idle succession game where you're the last maker of roasted barley tea, and your recipe rots a little every time you die unless you write it down.

ideas.title: Upstream
ideas.hook: A Satisfactory-style conveyor puzzle where you build belts to route commits between repos — splitters are branches, mergers are merges, and jams are merge conflicts.

ideas.title: Marked Deck
ideas.hook: Send the same document to five people and every copy carries a unique invisible fingerprint, so when one leaks you know exactly which card was marked.

ideas.title: Dowser
ideas.hook: A $20 mmWave radar board skinned as a mystical dowsing rod that actually finds studs, pipes, and water behind your walls by classifying material permittivity.

ideas.title: Mainspring
ideas.hook: A pull-back toy-car derby where you engineer the spring, gears, and wheels to nail a target distance — a child's toy tuned with the seriousness of a real drivetrain.

ideas.title: Cold Spot
ideas.hook: Phasmophobia for your laptop: something is haunting your battery, so grab your evidence tools, cross off the bestiary, and name the poltergeist process before it drains you.

ideas.title: Evict
ideas.hook: kubectl for your browser tabs — a real scheduler with liveness probes and memory-pressure eviction that reaps the 47 zombies you swore you'd read later.

ideas.title: The Director
ideas.hook: A roguelike whose difficulty scales with elapsed time — bolted onto your task list, so the monsters get nastier the longer you leave that thing rotting.

ideas.title: Cover Band
ideas.hook: Two functions that share zero characters yet pass the exact same tests — Cover Band breeds a whole family of look-nothing-alike twins from one seed and dares you to spot the doppelgängers.

ideas.title: Exploded View
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where you don't build machines — you tear them apart to deduce how they work, pulling a car back to watch the energy path light up through its guts.

ideas.title: Mothball
ideas.hook: A reverse city-builder where you don't grow a place — you carefully, expensively dismantle a dead nuclear plant without spreading contamination.

ideas.title: Deadmill
ideas.hook: A horror-escape game where your real treadmill pace is the only thing keeping the thing in the dark from catching you.

ideas.title: Cherry Pick
ideas.hook: You're customs at the border between two git repos, stamping each incoming commit APPROVE or REJECT while hunting for smuggled secrets and license contraband.

ideas.title: Last Round
ideas.hook: A GeoGuessr-meets-Pokémon-Go field game where you race other players to find, photograph, and 'claim' the last practitioners of dying local trades before they close for good.

ideas.title: Signal Séance
ideas.hook: A Phasmophobia-style co-op ghost hunt where the ghosts are the real Bluetooth and Wi-Fi devices haunting the room around you, and your 'EMF reader' is honest RF signal strength — so the party game is secretly a wardriving survey tool.

ideas.title: Brow Morse
ideas.hook: A webcam typing race where your only keys are your eyebrows — raise for dot, hold for dash — that's secretly a hands-free accessibility input method wearing a party-game costume.

ideas.title: Cost Golf
ideas.hook: A daily puzzle where you hand-tune indexes and rewrites to beat par on a real Postgres query plan, and the leaderboard is measured in milliseconds of EXPLAIN ANALYZE.

ideas.title: Compost
ideas.hook: Drop one image into a bin and walk away — an image model quietly re-eats its own output once a day for a year, slowly rotting your picture into abstract humus you can plant a new seed in.

ideas.title: Understudy
ideas.hook: A slow apprenticeship game where you learn a dying craft only by watching the last aging master — and any technique you fail to master before they pass is erased from your save forever.

ideas.title: Cutaway
ideas.hook: Paste a tiny function and watch it explode into an animated mechanical teardown diagram — registers as labeled parts, data flowing through the gears like a wound-up pull-back car.

ideas.title: Home Turf
ideas.hook: Generate a Project-Zomboid-style zombie-survival map of your actual neighborhood from real OpenStreetMap data, with your own house as the starting safehouse.

ideas.title: Sublevel
ideas.hook: An underground garden that only grows while you're away from the keyboard — touch a key and the sunlight burns your mushrooms.

ideas.title: Retinue
ideas.hook: Every self-hosted service on your homelab is a Rogue Trader crew member with a personality and a loyalty meter — let one crash and the whole bridge threatens mutiny.

ideas.title: Gridlock
ideas.hook: Your week is a city and your calendar is its traffic — book back-to-backs and watch agent-based congestion grind your day to a standstill.

ideas.title: Sandman
ideas.hook: Sleep badly and last night's Garmin data spawns a dungeon you cannot survive; sleep well and it hands you a floor full of loot.

ideas.title: Lobby Boss
ideas.hook: You don't play the match — you ARE the matchmaker, secretly stitching lobbies to keep whales happy, feed newbies winnable games, and quietly bury the sweatlords before everyone rage-quits.

ideas.title: Silent Typer
ideas.hook: A co-op party game where you can only 'speak' by tapping words a language model suggests — race to make your partner guess a secret phrase using nothing but a swipe-keyboard's prediction bar.

ideas.title: Star League
ideas.hook: Fantasy sports for GitHub Trending — draft a roster of hot repos on Monday, score points off the week's star velocity and releases, and trash-talk your friends when their AI framework flatlines.

ideas.title: Reboot Rings
ideas.hook: Every time you restart your computer, a living tree-ring wallpaper engraves one more ring — a year of your machine's life rendered as cross-cut wood you never sat down to make.

ideas.title: Fallow
ideas.hook: Your friendships are a greenhouse of houseplants that quietly wilt the longer you go without contact — and watering one means actually texting them.

ideas.title: Hunt & Peck
ideas.hook: One player types a famous phrase; everyone else sees only the audio waveform of their keystrokes and races to guess the words from rhythm alone.

ideas.title: House Tartan
ideas.hook: A tiny daemon weaves one thread of colour per day from your weather or resting heart rate — by New Year's you have a personal tartan you can actually get milled into a scarf.

ideas.title: Loot Ledger
ideas.hook: Import your bank statement and every purchase drops as ARPG loot — the $4 coffee is trash-grey common, the surprise vet bill a screen-shaking orange legendary.

ideas.title: Sanitize
ideas.hook: A Sekiro-style deflection duel where the attacks flying at you are real SQL-injection payloads and you parry each one with the correct defense in the timing window — miss, and a live in-browser database visibly leaks.

ideas.title: Thirty Baud
ideas.hook: A party game where you must describe a picture through a maddeningly low-bandwidth channel — no free typing, just nudging a predictive-text tree — so both players finally feel what brain-typing actually costs.

ideas.title: Will It Hover
ideas.hook: A brutally honest pre-build calculator that tells first-time drone builders 'this WON'T leave the ground' before they spend $400 finding out on the field.

ideas.title: Borrowed Time
ideas.hook: A heist-puzzle where the only way through a locked room is Rust's borrow checker — hand out many photocopies OR the one master key, never both.

ideas.title: Cruft
ideas.hook: A roguelike where you're the daemon-god of one server that must stay up — but every extra day of uptime spawns nastier memory-leak slimes, so your high score and your only escape hatch are the exact same button: reboot.

ideas.title: Greater Fool
ideas.hook: An Among Us-shaped party game where everyone trades a worthless fictional asset up a hidden curve — and the secret Insider, who knows the exact crash round, has to talk you into holding while they dump on your head.

ideas.title: Feast Day
ideas.hook: Every morning it hands you one real holiday happening somewhere on Earth today and a 30-second ritual to observe it; a year later you're holding a passport stamped with 300 celebrations you'd never have heard of.

ideas.title: Move Out
ideas.hook: A warehouse puzzle where you route loans of a crate to workers without ever crossing a mutable path with anything else — you're feeling the Rust borrow checker in your hands instead of reading its error messages.

ideas.title: Leftover Cartel
ideas.hook: Fridge management skinned as a clandestine supper-club empire — move your product before the heat (mold) busts you.

ideas.title: Horde Night
ideas.hook: A menubar survival game where your machine's uptime is the run, memory leaks are the zombies, and every seventh day without a reboot triggers a siege.

ideas.title: Want Gallery
ideas.hook: An adblocker run backwards: it deletes the article and hangs the ads on a museum wall with placards explaining who wants you and why.

ideas.title: Off Script
ideas.hook: A daily word game where you win by being the most surprising thing a language model still considers a sane sentence.

ideas.title: Vacuum Run
ideas.hook: Euro Truck Simulator's zen highway loop grafted onto Postgres: you cruise an autovacuum rig from table to table clearing dead tuples, and you can point it at your own database so real bloat becomes the road.

ideas.title: Zone Deck
ideas.hook: A roguelike deckbuilder where your deck is minted from your real Garmin/Strava workouts — intervals deal burst damage, rest days are the rare heal cards, and overtraining literally curses your hand.

ideas.title: Long Shutdown
ideas.hook: A reverse Cities: Skylines where you don't grow a mega-machine — you spend a decade safely decommissioning a particle collider, honoring real isotope half-lives so waiting becomes a legitimate move.

ideas.title: Star Bubble
ideas.hook: Mackay's 1852 'Madness of Crowds' as a game show: you're shown a trending repo's early star curve and bet how high the hype climbs before it pops.

ideas.title: Last Maker
ideas.hook: A cozy 'one more turn' management game where you're the final living practitioner of a genuinely endangered craft — pulled from real Red List data — racing your own old age to train an apprentice before the skill dies with you.

ideas.title: Daemon Farm
ideas.hook: Stardew Valley reskinned over your real machine: long-running processes grow like crops, zombie PIDs sprout as weeds, and rebooting is the harvest you've been guiltily avoiding all week.

ideas.title: Silent Sonar
ideas.hook: Turn any laptop into a hand-tracking radar using nothing but its speaker and mic, emitting an inaudible chirp and reading the echo — a $30k mmWave rig reincarnated as a browser tab.

ideas.title: Token Duel
ideas.hook: Read a real passage word by word and race a language model at guessing what comes next — then find out whether you or the machine is more predictable.

ideas.title: Endling
ideas.hook: Each round names a city and a dying trade — 'the only public bathhouse left' — and you race to pin the single survivor before the timer or your friends do.

ideas.title: Churn League
ideas.hook: Draft a roster of Steam games and score every week on their real concurrent-player swings — fantasy sports for the internet's attention span.

ideas.title: Invisible Ink
ideas.hook: Paste any AI-generated text and it x-rays the hidden watermarks — zero-width characters, statistical fingerprints, telltale unicode — then lets you strip them or forge your own.

ideas.title: Drop Rate
ideas.hook: Every purchase gets an ARPG loot-rarity roll — that $9 phone cable is a Legendary drop, the $200 gadget you used twice is gray vendor trash.

ideas.title: Beam Dump
ideas.hook: Shut down your homelab with the gravitas of CERN powering off the LHC — an orchestrated decommissioning ritual with a physicist's logbook.

ideas.title: Belt Budget
ideas.hook: Model your paycheck as a Satisfactory factory — income is ore on a conveyor, bills are machines that consume it, savings is the finished product, and you tune belt ratios until the bottleneck stops flashing red.

ideas.title: Query City
ideas.hook: Point it at your ad-blocker's DNS log and it grows an isometric city all year — busiest domains become the tallest towers, trackers pile up into a visibly smog-belching industrial district.

ideas.title: Tab Warband
ideas.hook: Your 87 open tabs are recruits in a Mount & Blade warband — the ones you actually use rise to elite, the ones you neglect lose morale and desert.

ideas.title: Cold Plan
ideas.hook: A daily duel where you're shown two SQL queries and must call which one the planner runs faster — before you're allowed to see a single EXPLAIN.

ideas.title: Windup Grand Prix
ideas.hook: You get a fixed spring budget, wind up a pull-back car, and let go — then chase the ghost of everyone who spent their coil differently on the same track.

ideas.title: Adblock Arena
ideas.hook: A speedrun where you race to nuke a page's ads and dark-patterns by writing filter rules faster and cleaner than anyone else on the leaderboard.

ideas.title: Heap Crawler
ideas.hook: Explore your live Postgres database as a Terraria-style minable cave where every 8KB page is a chunk, tuples are colored ore, and dead rows are the rubble of bloat waiting to be dug out.

ideas.title: Knock
ideas.hook: Tap any surface and your phone's mic tells you what's behind it — wood, metal, hollow drywall, or a stud — turning a research-grade material radar into a party trick that doubles as a stud finder.

ideas.title: Ghost Server
ideas.hook: A defibrillator for dying multiplayer games — find the ones down to their last hundred players and rally the survivors to log in at the same minute.

ideas.title: State Fragrance Map
ideas.hook: A US choropleth where every state reveals its most-searched scent and unfolds into a hoverable note pyramid.

ideas.title: Tap Water Card
ideas.hook: Type your ZIP and get a plain-language card of exactly what's swimming in your tap water.

ideas.title: Palette Drift
ideas.hook: Watch decades of game box art flow into a color-stream that proves the era when everything went brown.

ideas.title: Backlog Graveyard
ideas.hook: Your Steam library rendered as a graveyard of unplayed games, each tombstone engraved with its zero-hour epitaph.

ideas.title: Explorable Toy
ideas.hook: A single reactive explainer where dragging a number mid-sentence rewrites the conclusion and the diagram at once.

ideas.title: The pitch of a city
ideas.hook: Collide urban bird-song and traffic noise on one shared Hz axis, block by block, and see which species got squeezed out of the spectrum.

ideas.title: Prose tempo, read aloud
ideas.hook: Every English novel rendered as a 30-second TTS waveform, small-multiples wall from 1700 to 2020 — watch the rhythm get clipped.

ideas.title: Migration phase-shift clock
ideas.hook: Each tracked species on a polar 12-month clock with a ghost of its historical baseline — climate calendar-drift as a visible rotation.

ideas.title: IKEA name etymology atlas
ideas.hook: Trace every IKEA product name to its Nordic place-of-origin and watch the catalog migrate north over 70 years.

ideas.title: Fingerprint of a #1
ideas.hook: Every Billboard chart-topper as a single audio-fingerprint glyph via Essentia; decade grids reveal the slow death of dynamic range.

ideas.title: Word-of-the-year by smell
ideas.hook: 150 years of US newspapers as a sparkline grid of scent adjectives — visualizing what the country's nose was paying attention to.

ideas.title: Aftershock metronome
ideas.hook: Every M5+ quake since 1900 as a tick on a clock that spins once per century — seismic clustering becomes a visible heartbeat.

ideas.title: Patent drawing entropy
ideas.hook: Edge density of the lead figure on every US utility patent, 1836 to today — watch drawings get busier through WWII, transistor, software eras.

ideas.title: Species ledger
ideas.hook: IUCN Red List status changes as a time-axis Sankey — watch taxa migrate LC to EX across the decades, a ledger of loss drawn as flow.

ideas.title: CPI component drift
ideas.hook: The 200+ items in the CPI basket as a sparkline wall sorted by peak decade — watch payphones and film developing get retired from the cart.

ideas.title: Dollar General openings × rural hospital closures
ideas.hook: County-level 2010–2024: which arrives first, the dollar store or the hospital closure — and does the lead/lag flip by region? One map that ends an argument.

ideas.title: Light pollution × astronomy-club density
ideas.hook: NASA VIIRS night-lights under astronomy-club halos: where is the sky still worth driving to, and how far is the drive from where the stargazers actually live?

ideas.title: The golden-retriever line
ideas.hook: A concentric breed gradient keyed to lot size: frenchies in the core, goldens in the donut, labs on the fringe. The suburban ring, decoded by dog.

ideas.title: Superfund half-life
ideas.hook: How long does a town remember its poison? Per-region decay curves of home-price recovery near EPA Superfund sites — with the diff-in-diff honesty the finding demands.

ideas.title: Cemetery surname half-life
ideas.hook: Ethnicity has a half-life measured in headstones: OSM cemetery polygons × ACS surnames reveal the gap between who's buried here and who lives here now.

ideas.title: What's been seen from this exact bench
ideas.hook: Paste a lat/lon, get a 50m-radius species pillar of every fungus, lichen, beetle, and bird ever logged at arm's reach — scrubbable by season, shareable as one PNG.

ideas.title: Half-life of your kitchen
ideas.hook: Tag the objects in one room and re-render the scene as a timeline tower: banana peel at the bottom (2 weeks), granite countertop towering into geologic time.

ideas.title: Gaia parallax porch
ideas.hook: Enter your address and birthdate; see the night sky over your porch with each star annotated by how far it has drifted since you were born.

ideas.title: Mycelium commute
ideas.hook: Race the fastest documented fungus at its real cm/day rate along your actual commute route. 'Your bus beat the honey fungus by 4 hours.'

ideas.title: Mineral inventory of one human
ideas.hook: A body silhouette laced with ribbons to real mines: your iron to Carajás, your phosphorus to Bou Craa — the ~60 elements you're made of, sourced to specific pits on Earth.

ideas.title: Stage Direction Atlas
ideas.hook: Every playwright's stage directions plotted as verb-class density across the play — Beckett is pauses, Mamet is exits, Ibsen is furniture, Shakespeare barely directs. The shape of a playwright's silence.

ideas.title: Loanword Arrival Map
ideas.hook: English borrowings by donor language and semantic domain, animated across nine centuries — French floods law and food 1200-1400, Italian owns 1600s music, Japanese owns 1980s tech.

ideas.title: SCOTUS Dissent Prosody
ideas.hook: Do justices write angrier when they lose? Pair each justice's majority vs dissent prosody — sentence length and clause depth — and watch Scalia's dissents spike.

ideas.title: Recipe Imperative Drift
ideas.hook: Cookbooks 1800-2020 measured for imperatives vs prose and step length — pre-1900 recipes are essays, post-1990 they're command-line scripts. Watch the sentence contract.

ideas.title: Wikipedia Revert-War Lexicon
ideas.hook: Edit summaries from the most-reverted articles, clustered by rhetorical move — 'per WP:RS,' 'rv vandalism,' 'see talk.' A sociolinguistic map of gatekeeping in four-word phrases.

ideas.title: Mohs the Page
ideas.hook: Drag a diamond across your screen and watch it gouge talc — the scratch IS the encoding.

ideas.title: True Spectrum
ideas.hook: The honest visible spectrum via CIE 1931 — not a fake rainbow — with animal vision bands and pitch on hover.

ideas.title: Pressure Atlas
ideas.hook: Press and hold to compare every sport's ball-at-impact contact pressure on one scale.

ideas.title: Viscosity Drip
ideas.hook: Eighty liquids dripping at true relative viscosity — scrub a temperature slider and watch honey thin.

ideas.title: Piano in Pascals
ideas.hook: All 88 keys of a Steinway D with true string length, tension, and SPL — click to hear it and see the string vibrate at f/100.

ideas.title: Overdue
ideas.hook: Personal finance as Dead by Daylight — your fixed monthly bills are the Killer stalking you across the map, and every payday you sprint between generators (rent, card, subscriptions) frantically repairing them before the 1st of the month, when the Killer hooks whatever you didn't finish.

ideas.title: Strata
ideas.hook: A geologic core sample of your own machine — every day it silently deposits one 2px sediment layer colored by that day's dominant app, CPU heat, and idle time, so a year later you can read your life as rock strata and mine it for the week everything went sideways.

ideas.title: Freight
ideas.hook: Your homelab backups become Euro Truck Simulator long-hauls — every rsync job is a cargo truck grinding down a real-time highway, thermal throttling shows up as driver fatigue, and a failed transfer is a jackknifed rig on the shoulder.

ideas.title: Echo Field
ideas.hook: A survival game where you can't tell what anything is made of by looking — you carry a handheld mmWave radar and must read each object's echo signature to know if that berry, that beam, or that pool is food, fuel, or poison.

ideas.title: Pull
ideas.hook: A drag-toy that models a real pull-back car — wind the spring by dragging back (true Hooke's-law energy stored), let go, and watch the stored joules convert to distance across surfaces with honest friction, where over-winding makes it flip instead of go.

ideas.title: Zero-Width
ideas.hook: A party word-game where one player smuggles a secret word into an ordinary group chat using invisible steganographic characters, and everyone else — reading the same visible text — has to sniff out who's hiding a signal and what it says.

ideas.title: Uptime Rings
ideas.hook: A tiny app that turns your Mac's own reboot, sleep, and wake log into a slowly-growing tree-ring mandala — one full ring per year, one scar per crash, so your machine quietly draws a portrait of its own life while you use it.

ideas.title: Sell The Top
ideas.hook: A daily Wordle-style game where you ride a real historical mania — tulips, South Sea, dotcom — and try to sell before the crowd, then get graded against how the actual 1637 crowd behaved.

ideas.title: Canary Text
ideas.hook: Paste your group-chat drama through it and each friend gets an invisibly fingerprinted copy — so when it leaks, the zero-width characters rat out exactly who screenshotted.

ideas.title: Uptime Familiar
ideas.hook: A menubar creature that feeds on your machine's uptime — it grows fat and strange the longer you refuse to restart, and rebooting is euthanasia.

ideas.title: Divvy
ideas.hook: An idea cloud that grows itself and writes a PRD for every bubble.

ideas.title: Show HN Graveyard
ideas.hook: Track every 'Show HN' launch and chart how many are still alive a year later.

ideas.title: Changelog Roguelike
ideas.hook: A dungeon crawler where each floor is generated from a real open-source project's git history.

ideas.title: Front-Page Mood Ring
ideas.hook: One color per day, derived from the sentiment of the HN front page. A year = a stripe painting.

ideas.title: Homelab Tycoon
ideas.hook: An idle game where you build a self-hosted stack and fight entropy: cert expiries, stale mounts, OOM kills.
